title: Energy Source Comparison Dashboard
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive radar chart comparing 8 energy sources across EROI, emissions, land use, reliability, cost, and scalability

learningObjectives: Compare energy sources across multiple criteria to evaluate trade-offs
Interpret radar charts as a tool for multi-dimensional data comparison
Explain why no single energy source is optimal across all dimensions
prerequisites: Basic understanding of renewable vs. nonrenewable energy
Familiarity with CO2 emissions and climate change
Ability to read charts and graphs

keyQuestions: Which energy source scores highest on the most dimensions?
Why is there no single 'best' energy source?
How does EROI affect the long-term viability of an energy source?
What trade-offs exist between cost and environmental impact?
commonMisconceptions: Students often believe one energy source is clearly best across all criteria
Students may not realize that low cost can mask high environmental externalities
Students may assume renewable always means zero environmental impact
assessmentOpportunities: Students complete pairwise comparison worksheets using dashboard data
Students write a recommendation memo citing specific data points
Class discussion on why different countries make different energy choices
